context.description: Philosophical argument about dream logic versus waking consciousness, using shared observation to question subjective self-understanding. It contrasts science’s success in natural inquiry with the persistence of self-protective subjectivity and turns back to the broader question of what can be known about being.
normalization_notes: Raw category and subcategory mapped to the closest allowed book body content / theoretical discussion codes.
Document title inferred from source file name.

rows.paragraph_text: Argument about dream logic, shared perception, and the limits of philosophy without waking consciousness.
Shared observations are proposed as the only reliable basis for discussion in the dream world.
Critique of subjective self-understanding, contrasting science's success with fear of exposing contradictions.
Return to the question of whether comparisons and speculation about inaccessible realms are justified.
